methods.description: Process for selecting specific sampling sites within the defined study regions based on geographical location, stream order, and data availability criteria to ensure representation of different stream sizes and network positions.

steps.description: Identify three geographically distant regions: the Irtysh River (ITR), the middle section of Qiantang River (QTR), and the upper section of the Mekong River (MKR) in China.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify study regions based on geographical criteria.

steps.description: Focus investigation on streams ranging from first to fourth Strahler orders within the selected regions.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter streams by Strahler order.

steps.description: Classify orders 1-2 as headwater sites and orders 3-4 as downstream sites.
steps.searchable_summary: Classify sites into headwater or downstream categories based on stream order.
outputs: Sites classified as headwater or downstream

steps.description: Select the same number of headwater (n = 15) and downstream (n = 15) sites within each region based on two restrictions: (1) included sites where both macroinvertebrates and diatoms were collected, and (2) included headwater sites that had the highest position in the river network, and downstream sites that had the lowest position in the river network.
steps.searchable_summary: Select specific sites based on data availability and network position criteria.
inputs: Data availability status (macroinvertebrates, diatoms) for each site
Desired number of sites per category/region (n=15 headwater, n=15 downstream)

steps.description: Verify that selected headwater sites were significantly (p < 0.0001) narrower and shallower than downstream sites within each region.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform statistical test to compare physical characteristics (width, depth) between selected headwater and downstream sites.

steps.description: Conduct all analyses using only the selected sites in each region.
steps.searchable_summary: Define the dataset for subsequent analyses using only the selected sites.
inputs: Full dataset of potential sites and associated data

methods.description: Procedure for collecting benthic macroinvertebrate samples from stream reaches, preserving them, and processing them in the laboratory for identification and counting.

steps.description: Collect benthic macroinvertebrates from a 100 m-long reach at each sampling site.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect macroinvertebrate samples from defined stream reaches.

steps.description: Collect macroinvertebrates using a Surber-net (30 × 30 cm, 250 µm mesh size) from three riffles and two pools with a total of 0.45 m² sampling area.
steps.searchable_summary: Sample specific habitats (riffles, pools) within the reach using a Surber net.
inputs: Number of samples per habitat (3 riffles, 2 pools)

steps.description: Combine all Surber net samples into one composite sample.
steps.searchable_summary: Combine multiple sub-samples into a single composite sample.

steps.description: Preserve the composite sample in 10% buffered formalin.
steps.searchable_summary: Preserve the composite sample using a chemical fixative.

steps.description: In the laboratory, sort macroinvertebrate individuals from the sample.
steps.searchable_summary: Separate macroinvertebrate individuals from debris in the sample.

steps.description: Count the sorted macroinvertebrate individuals.
steps.searchable_summary: Count the number of individuals in the sample.

steps.description: Identify macroinvertebrate individuals to the lowest practical taxonomic level, in most case to genus (>75% of taxa).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify macroinvertebrate individuals to the lowest possible taxonomic level.

steps.description: Presence-absence data for macroinvertebrates are provided in the Supplementary Material Data Sheet 2.
steps.searchable_summary: Generate presence-absence data for macroinvertebrates.
outputs: Macroinvertebrate presence-absence data (Supplementary Material Data Sheet 2)

methods.description: Procedure for collecting diatom samples from stream substrates, processing them in the field and laboratory for identification and counting.

steps.description: Collect diatoms from nine transects at each site.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect diatom samples from multiple transects within a site.

steps.description: Scrape diatoms off one coarse substrate particle from a defined area (10.17 cm²) with a toothbrush and an area delimiter (PVC tube) at each transect.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect diatoms from a defined area on a substrate particle using specific tools.

steps.description: Wash and combine the nine subsamples into a single composite sample.
steps.searchable_summary: Wash and combine multiple subsamples into a single composite sample.

steps.description: Add distilled water to a constant volume of 500 ml to the composite sample.
steps.searchable_summary: Adjust the volume of the composite sample using distilled water.
outputs: Composite diatom sample at target volume

steps.description: Extract 50 ml out of the 500 ml to a specimen bottle for taxonomic analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Take a subsample from the composite sample for laboratory analysis.
outputs: Diatom subsample (50 ml) in a specimen bottle

steps.description: Preserve the 50 ml sample by adding two ml of 10% formalin.
steps.searchable_summary: Preserve the subsample using a chemical fixative.

steps.description: In the laboratory, a total of 500 frustules per sample were identified and counted with a light microscope (Olympus BX41TF) at 1,000 × magnification.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and count diatom frustules under a microscope.
outputs: Taxonomic identification and count data for diatoms

steps.description: All diatom individuals were identified to the [lowest practical taxonomic level, implied by references]. Presence-absence data for diatoms are provided in the Supplementary Material Data Sheet 3.
steps.searchable_summary: Generate presence-absence data for diatoms.
outputs: Diatom presence-absence data (Supplementary Material Data Sheet 3)
